meta.title: Mariology 2: Assessing the Akathist Hymn and Responding to Common Eastern Orthodox Rejoinders
meta.author: Joshua Schooping

questions.question: According to the speaker, what does failing to tell inquirers early—that those who refuse to bow and kiss icons are anathema per the Seventh Ecumenical Council—illustrate?
options: A complete history of Byzantine monasticism
Soft selling the force of Orthodox teaching on icons
A required step before baptism in all jurisdictions
A purely optional local custom
questions.answer: Soft selling the force of Orthodox teaching on icons

questions.question: How does the speaker distinguish lying from what he thinks is happening in many apologetic conversations?
options: He claims all Orthodox teachers knowingly falsify doctrine
He equates silence with lying
He says deceived people can teach what they believe without deliberately lying
He denies that sincerity matters in theology
questions.answer: He says deceived people can teach what they believe without deliberately lying

questions.question: The 17th-century Jerusalem Council (as discussed) is used to argue which stark claim about non–Eastern Orthodox Christians?
options: They are fully united in sacramental grace
They are technically not Christians on the formal conciliar reading described
They are preferred dialogue partners over Catholics
They are identical to catechumens
questions.answer: They are technically not Christians on the formal conciliar reading described

questions.question: Why does the speaker reject “it’s poetry / genre” as a full defense of Marian prayer language?
options: He denies that hymns exist in Orthodoxy
He argues prayer should still be humble and truthful, so hyperbole is not a blank check
He thinks only prose may be used in worship
He claims the Bible contains no poetry
questions.answer: He argues prayer should still be humble and truthful, so hyperbole is not a blank check

questions.question: Regarding “all my hope in Mary” next to a Trinitarian prayer, what does the speaker say is the real problem?
options: A trivial translation error
A logical contradiction that disproves the Trinity
A confusion that invests Mary with properties proper to Christ, not merely a contradiction
Proof that Orthodox theology rejects Mary’s intercession
questions.answer: A confusion that invests Mary with properties proper to Christ, not merely a contradiction

questions.question: When Orthodox defenders call exclusive Marian intercessory language hyperbolic “desperate need,” what New Testament counter does the speaker raise?
options: There is one mediator between God and man, the man Christ Jesus
Mary’s Magnificat forbids all prayer to her
The book of Acts bans evening prayer
Desperate need must always be expressed in Aramaic
questions.answer: There is one mediator between God and man, the man Christ Jesus

questions.question: Why does the speaker dismiss the Obi-Wan / Star Wars “only hope” analogy for Marian prayer?
options: He thinks Star Wars is doctrinally authoritative
He argues secular fiction is a poor index for solemn, saint-attributed prayer language
He confuses Obi-Wan with Chrysostom
He claims Leia was addressing the Theotokos
questions.answer: He argues secular fiction is a poor index for solemn, saint-attributed prayer language

questions.question: What is the speaker’s objection to softening “only hope” into “it would be a big help to have her prayers”?
options: He denies Mary can pray for anyone
He says it misrepresents prayers that frame stronger refusal-to-go-to-Jesus language
He thinks intercession is only for clergy
He requires all prayers to be addressed to angels
questions.answer: He says it misrepresents prayers that frame stronger refusal-to-go-to-Jesus language

questions.question: Which prayer location does the speaker cite before the Akathist (attributed to John Chrysostom)?
options: Page 154 in the prayer book from the press named in the transcript as saint econ’s monastery press
The first page of the Septuagint
An appendix only in Slavonic manuscripts
A 21st-century blog post
questions.answer: Page 154 in the prayer book from the press named in the transcript as saint econ’s monastery press

questions.question: What phrase from that pre-communion prayer does the speaker highlight as soteriological language applied to Mary?
options: Who is my only unassailable hope, intercession, and salvation
Who is a helpful example of patience
Who is mentioned only in a footnote
Who is identical to the Holy Spirit
questions.answer: Who is my only unassailable hope, intercession, and salvation

questions.question: How does the speaker characterize the Akathist to the Theotokos in Orthodox practice?
options: An obscure monastic curiosity
The most popular Akathist, sung in churches and homes—core, not marginal
A text rejected by all bishops
A Protestant composition
questions.answer: The most popular Akathist, sung in churches and homes—core, not marginal

questions.question: What reductio does the speaker use against “through thee the curse shall cease” logic?
options: Mary’s parents—and ultimately Adam—would get credit for new creation by the same reasoning
Curses never end in biblical theology
Only priests may read the Akathist
The curse is unrelated to Christ in any tradition
questions.answer: Mary’s parents—and ultimately Adam—would get credit for new creation by the same reasoning

questions.question: In the Akathist discussion, how does the speaker describe Mary being called “wisdom surpassing the wise”?
options: As saying she had ordinary good advice
As identifying her with wisdom in a way that parallels Christ-language
As denying Christ is wisdom
As a quote from the Council of Trent
questions.answer: As identifying her with wisdom in a way that parallels Christ-language

questions.question: Which Akathist line does the speaker single out as ascribing propitiation to Mary?
options: Rejoice, propitiation for the whole world
Rejoice, keeper of the church budget
Rejoice, author of the book of Romans
Rejoice, replacement for the Eucharist
questions.answer: Rejoice, propitiation for the whole world

questions.question: What does “freedom of approach for mortals unto God” in the Akathist signify for the speaker’s critique?
options: It is grounds for iconoclasm
It centers access on Mary rather than Christ’s cross and heavenly ministry, as he reads it
It denies Mary any role
It refers only to Old Testament temple taxes
questions.answer: It centers access on Mary rather than Christ’s cross and heavenly ministry, as he reads it

questions.question: Who does the speaker say is the true “ladder” between earth and heaven in his Christological contrast?
options: John the Baptist
Christ
The high priest alone
The emperor Constantine
questions.answer: Christ

questions.question: Why does the speaker object to Mary as “beam of the spiritual sun”?
options: He rejects all metaphors in theology
He reads it as blurring creature and uncreated light / divine platform imagery
He thinks the sun is a pagan deity only
He believes Mary was not part of the Incarnation
questions.answer: He reads it as blurring creature and uncreated light / divine platform imagery

questions.question: Which image does the speaker use for Mary taking credit for Christ’s accomplishments?
options: Spiritual helicopter mom
Absentee landlord
Roman consul
Desert father without disciples
questions.answer: Spiritual helicopter mom

questions.question: What problem does the speaker raise about ending a Marian kontakion with “Alleluia” directed to Mary?
options: Alleluia has no historical use in Christian worship
The term includes Yah; addressing it to Mary risks confusion with the Godhead
Orthodox never sing Alleluia
Mary is forbidden from praise in any form
questions.answer: The term includes Yah; addressing it to Mary risks confusion with the Godhead

questions.question: Which Scripture does the speaker cite for standing on the sufficiency of Scripture at the close?
options: 2 Timothy 3:16–17
Psalm 119:1 only
Revelation 22:18–19 only
Matthew 1:1
questions.answer: 2 Timothy 3:16–17

questions.question: What does the speaker say causes cognitive dissonance alongside “steeping” people in liturgical services?
options: Apologetic qualifiers that do not match what services repeatedly say
Too few icons in parishes
Exclusive use of Latin
Banning the Akathist
questions.answer: Apologetic qualifiers that do not match what services repeatedly say

questions.question: The golden calf episode is used to argue what about “we didn’t mean it” defenses?
options: That Israel intended another god outright
That hyperbolic intent justified the calf
That inappropriate devotion cannot be excused merely as poetic elevation
That Moses endorsed the calf
questions.answer: That inappropriate devotion cannot be excused merely as poetic elevation

questions.question: According to the speaker, what formal consequence follows from rejecting the Jerusalem Council while claiming conciliar infallibility?
options: It strengthens ecumenical dialogue automatically
It creates tension with the church’s reception of that council as legitimate
It is required by the Seventh Council
It was mandated by Photios
questions.answer: It creates tension with the church’s reception of that council as legitimate

questions.question: What does the speaker claim Orthodox piety sometimes discourages regarding Christ?
options: Going to Christ as intercessor in favor of Mary as chief intercessor
Reading the Gospels
Baptizing infants
Using incense
questions.answer: Going to Christ as intercessor in favor of Mary as chief intercessor

questions.question: How does the speaker describe Isaiah’s mountains singing compared to Marian propitiation language?
options: They are identical claims
The Isaianic poetry paints a different intended sense than calling Mary propitiation
Isaiah forbids all poetry
Mountains literally sing in the New Earth only
questions.answer: The Isaianic poetry paints a different intended sense than calling Mary propitiation
